beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and make coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to the day; morning routine before heading to work.

beats.activity: Commute from home to SoFi office via car, avoiding Light Rail closure.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closed Westlake–UW until 3pm; driving via surface streets avoids the outage.

beats.activity: Deep work on backend feature development at SoFi.
beats.reasoning: Solid morning coding block before the FIFA match; fintech work doesn't require meetings early.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match on break at office.
beats.reasoning: World Cup match airs at 11am; Shaan watches it live at the office break room rather than missing it.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby cafe.
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch walk from downtown office; takes advantage of sunny weather and proximity to market.

beats.activity: Walk back to office after lunch.
beats.reasoning: Return to workplace; Pike Place is walkable from downtown SoFi office.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: code review, team standup, and feature implementation.
beats.reasoning: Productive afternoon block; typical fintech engineer workflow with meetings and coding.

beats.activity: Commute home from SoFi office by car.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail still closed until 3pm, so drove this morning; drive home as well to avoid surface street congestion at rush hour.

beats.activity: Pick up groceries and household items on the way home.
beats.reasoning: Convenient stop en route; restocking for the week in sunny weather.

beats.activity: Finish commute home and put away groceries.
beats.reasoning: Complete the journey home from Ballard Safeway.

diary: Started the day with my usual routine—coffee in Magnolia hit different this morning, maybe because the sun was actually out. Drove downtown to avoid the Light Rail mess; glad I checked the transit alerts. Got some solid backend work done before the World Cup match, which was the highlight honestly. USA vs Wales had me glued to my monitor for an hour and a half. The energy in the office was pretty fun, everyone gathered around. Grabbed piroshkies at Pike Place after—those always hit the spot. The walk back cleared my head before diving into code reviews and the afternoon grind. Long day at SoFi, but productive. Swung by Safeway in Ballard on the way home to restock, and now I'm just decompressing. Tired but satisfied.
thought_process: The light rail closure actually worked in my favor—I got to sleep an extra ten minutes by driving instead of worrying about transit timing. Work was flowing until the World Cup match, which I definitely needed; the break recharged me mentally. I chose to walk back from Pike Place instead of taking a rideshare because the weather was perfect and I wanted to decompress before jumping back into code reviews. The afternoon felt heavier with meetings and debugging, so I kept my head down. Grocery stop in Ballard was practical—I've been meaning to restock all week. Overall, a solid Monday that balanced focus with some downtime. The sunny weather definitely lifted my mood throughout the day.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
